Mono erases type variables and bakes a"] # [doc = " data type's arguments into its **mangled-nullary** name, so an applied `Ty::Data(_, args!=[])`"] # [doc = " becomes the `Named` of its mangled name; a `Ty::Var` would be an internal error, surfaced as a"] # [doc = " distinctive `Named` so a leak is never silent (rather than a panic)."] pub (crate) fn ty_to_ref (t : & Ty) -> TypeRef { let base = match t { Ty :: Binary (Width :: Lit (n)) => BaseType :: Binary (WidthRef :: Lit (* n)) , Ty :: Binary (Width :: Var (v)) => BaseType :: Binary (WidthRef :: Name (v . clone ())) , Ty :: Ternary (Width :: Lit (m)) => BaseType :: Ternary (WidthRef :: Lit (* m)) , Ty :: Ternary (Width :: Var (v)) => BaseType :: Ternary (WidthRef :: Name (v . clone ())) , Ty :: Dense (d , s) => BaseType :: Dense (* d , * s) , Ty :: Vsa { model , dim , sparsity , } => BaseType :: Vsa { model : model . clone () , dim : * dim , sparsity : sparsity . clone () , } , Ty :: Substrate (tag) => BaseType :: Substrate (tag . clone ()) , Ty :: Seq (elem , n) => BaseType :: Seq { elem : Box :: new (ty_to_ref (elem)) , len : * n , } , Ty :: Bytes => BaseType :: Bytes , Ty :: Float => BaseType :: Float , Ty :: Data (n , args) if args . is_empty () => BaseType :: Named (mangle_safe_name (n) , vec ! []) , Ty :: Data (_ , _) => BaseType :: Named (mangle_ty (t) , vec ! []) , Ty :: Var (v) => BaseType :: Named (format ! ("VAR_{v}") , vec ! []) , Ty :: Fn (a , r) => BaseType :: Fn (Box :: new (ty_to_ref (a)) , Box :: new (ty_to_ref (r))) , } ; TypeRef :: unguaranteed (base) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Like [`ty_to_ref`], but attaches `guarantee` — the **source declaration's own** `@ g` (M-844 /"] # [doc = " M-967; RFC-0018 §4). Every call site passes the guarantee straight off the *original* (still"] # [doc = " abstract/generic) [`TypeRef`] being specialized (e.g. `p.ty.guarantee`, `fd.sig.ret.guarantee`)"] # [doc = " — never derived from the concrete `Ty` (which carries none) and never merged/averaged across"] # [doc = " instantiations (each call builds one fresh `TypeRef` for one specialization). This is the sole"] # [doc = " per-instantiation guarantee-tag threading point: a monomorphized copy's signature/ascription"] # [doc = " keeps exactly what its own source wrote — no silent loss (the pre-M-967 `ty_to_ref` blanking),"] # [doc = " no silent merge across instantiations, and no upgrade past the source's own annotation (VR-5)."] pub (crate) fn ty_to_ref_tagged (t : & Ty , guarantee : Option < Strength >) -> TypeRef { let mut r = ty_to_ref (t) ; r . guarantee = guarantee ; r }
